behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module handoff (CSV row index -> validator -> DLQ payload), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires rows to appear in DLQ with row number, not real S3/DB/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser, schema validator, and DLQ writer in-process. Feed a small CSV with known missing required fields and assert DLQ records include exact original row numbers and rejected row payloads.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is transformation logic for date parsing/normalization and can be reproduced by direct function input/output tests. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require cross-module state handoff; it is a pure conversion rule.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European strings (including edge cases) and assert exact canonical output and parse failures for invalid dates.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: timeout and memory-exhaustion are runtime/OS-level failure modes; in-process mocks hide real allocation/backpressure behavior. MOCK-HIDE: fake sinks/clients can pass while real process OOMs or breaches timeout. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline as a real process against a 500k-row fixture, with production-like memory limits and timeout config. Measure wall-clock completion and peak RSS; fail if timeout exceeded or process exits from memory pressure.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior references real HTTP 503 semantics from S3; minimum reliable boundary is networked client behavior with status/error translation and retry timing. MOCK-HIDE: mocking S3 client can miss SDK transport/error-mapping and retry timing interactions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Execute pipeline against an S3-compatible test endpoint (or HTTP fault proxy) that returns 503 for initial requests then succeeds. Assert batch completes and observed retry intervals follow configured exponential backoff bounds.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requires real PostgreSQL write semantics plus restart behavior (process stop/start and persisted progress/idempotency state). MOCK-HIDE: mocked DB or no real restart can miss duplicate insert races and transaction boundary issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Start pipeline against real PostgreSQL, inject a controlled crash/restart mid-batch, then resume processing. Verify final table contains each logical row exactly once (using unique keys/checksums) with no duplicates.
